question:
An entrepreneurer of start-up company calls potential customers to sale a new
product. Assume that the outcomes of consecutive calls are independent and
that on each call he has 20% chance of making a sale. His daily goal is to make
5 sales and he can make only 25 calls in a day. What is the probability that he
achieves his goal in between 15 and 20 trials?
answer: Let $Y$ be the number of trials up to and including the 5th success. $P(15 \leq Y \leq$ 20) $=0.2405$.
